summary: Simon and Eve took a weekend break to Halse Farm campsite on Exmoor (16-18 October 2009), driving down the M5 past Bristol. On Saturday they walked 4.75 miles along Winsford Hill and down to Winsford village for lunch at the Royal Oak Inn; on Sunday they stopped at an engine house in the Brendon Hills before returning home.
extraction_notes: Only two diary pages (B_0168 and B_0169) were in the bundle; B_0169_L ends mid-sentence and is marked boundary_mixed, suggesting the trip may continue onto further pages. 'Queens Camel' appears in the title_guess but is absent from all transcribed pages — it is likely on a subsequent page. The dog is transcribed as 'Tonic' but context identifies the family dog as 'Tinder'. No sea crossings: entirely within England. Brandon Hills is the diary's spelling for Brendon Hills (Somerset mining area with historic engine houses).
